risk_rationale: First subtask in engine/catalog doing real file I/O (os.File open/create/WriteAt/Sync/Truncate) rather than pure in-memory byte-buffer logic like record.go/page.go.

Introduces on-disk durability concerns (free-list persistence across process restart) that engine/wal/ will later formalize with a proper write-ahead log; this subtask's Sync-on-write is a stopgap, not a WAL-backed guarantee.

Still single-threaded: FileManager has no internal locking and is not safe for concurrent use from multiple goroutines. Striped-mutex CRUD (1.1.5) and the fileID allocator (1.1.4) are separate, later subtasks -- this subtask must not introduce any locking, atomics-based ID allocation, or CRUD API.

Filesystem-dependent behavior (sparse files, WriteAt semantics, Sync cost) can behave subtly differently across OSes/CI runners; mitigated by using t.TempDir() in tests and avoiding any hardcoded paths.

impacted_modules.description: FileManager type: Open/Close, AllocatePage, FreePage, ReadPage, WritePage, bitmap free-list on page 0.
TestCatalogFileManager: create fresh file, allocate N, free some, reallocate (reuse), reopen and verify persisted state.

out_of_scope_confirmed: fileID allocator (atomic counter, monotonic, no reuse) -- belongs to 1.1.4 (idalloc.go)
CRUD API / striped locking -- belongs to 1.1.5 (catalog.go)
Full WAL-backed durability -- belongs to engine/wal/ (separate later phase)
Record-delete-triggers-page-free end-to-end flow spanning Page.DeleteSlot + catalog CRUD -- FreePage is exercised directly by the test to simulate that scenario, per the subtask spec
rollback_plan: Single new-file-only change (file.go + file_test.go); revert via git revert of the single commit with no cross-module fallout since no other module yet imports FileManager.
